questions.q: The HCF of 96 and 404 using Euclid's Division Algorithm is:
According to the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, every composite number can be expressed as a product of primes in:
options: A unique way, apart from the order of factors
questions.explain: The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic guarantees that the prime factorization of any composite number is unique, except for the order in which the prime factors occur.
Using HCF × LCM = Product of the numbers: 13 × LCM = 26 × 91 = 2366, so LCM = 2366 ÷ 13 = 182.

questions.q: Which of the following is an irrational number?
questions.explain: √5 cannot be expressed as p/q where p, q are integers and q ≠ 0, so it is irrational. √16 = 4 (rational), 3/7 and 0.333... are both rational.

questions.q: The LCM of two numbers is 1200. Which of the following cannot be their HCF?
questions.explain: HCF must be a perfect factor of LCM. 1200 is not divisible by 500, so 500 cannot be the HCF.

questions.q: If n is any natural number, then 6ⁿ - 5ⁿ always ends with the digit:
questions.explain: 6ⁿ always ends in 6 and 5ⁿ always ends in 5, so their difference always ends in 1.

questions.q: The product of a non-zero rational number and an irrational number is always:
questions.explain: The product is always guaranteed to be irrational.

questions.q: If the HCF of 65 and 117 is expressible in the form 65m - 117, then the value of m is:
